news.summary: A press event was held in Columbus, Ohio to announce the 2025 Forbes Under 30 Summit in partnership with JobsOhio, along with the introduction of the Forbes 30 Under 30 Local Columbus List. The summit is scheduled to be held in Columbus from September 28 to October 1, 2025, bringing together young leaders, founders, and creators from around the world for networking and collaboration. This event aims to foster connections, facilitate learning opportunities, and inspire innovation among the participants.
news.headline: Forbes 2025 Under 30 Summit in Columbus, Ohio to feature 3-time Olympic medalist Suni Lee, Vivek Ramaswamy & more.

news.summary: Best Buy Co Inc (NYSE: BBY) experienced a decline in trading on Thursday after reporting its first-quarter 2026 earnings that were below expectations. The company's first-quarter sales dropped by approximately 1% (with comparable sales falling by 0.7%) year-over-year to $8.77 billion which missed the analyst consensus of $9.22 billion. Domestic revenue stood at $8.13 billion, showing a 0.9% decline mainly due to a 0.7% drop in comparable sales. The main contributors to the decline in comparable sales were home theater, appliances, and drones, although this was partially offset by growth in computing, mobile phone, and tablet categories. Domestic online revenue increased by 2.1% to $2.58 billion.
news.headline: Best Buy misses Q1 2026 earnings expectations with 1% drop in sales to $8.77 billion; domestic revenue fell 0.9%.

news.summary: Shares of C3.Ai Inc (NYSE: AI) surged after the company exceeded expectations in its fourth-quarter report. The U.S. Air Force increased their contract ceiling with the company to $450 million. C3 AI reported Q4 revenue of $108.7 million, surpassing estimates of $107.73 million. They also reported an adjusted loss of 18 cents per share, beating the expected 20 cents per share loss. Total revenue saw a 26% increase year-over-year, with subscription revenue up 9%. The company highlighted a renewed and expanded partnership with Baker Hughes through a multi-year deal. C3 AI closed the quarter with $742.7 million.
news.headline: C3 AI stock soars as Q4 revenue hits $108.7M, beating estimates. U.S. Air Force raises contract ceiling to $450M. Renewed partnership with Baker Hughes.

news.summary: The article discusses the adoption and innovation surrounding blockchain technology in Africa, as opposed to the focus on speculation and regulation in the West. Kevin Imani from StarkWare’s Africa Venture Studio highlights how blockchain is being used to address real-world problems in finance, energy, and connectivity across the continent. Early adoption was driven by local needs, with students in countries like Kenya and Nigeria using blockchain for financial transactions due to limited banking infrastructure and trust in government institutions. African innovators are also using blockchain for energy solutions, with surplus power from mini hydro stations being used to mine Bitcoin and create sustainable power loops. Additionally, blockchain is being utilized for decentralized WiFi networks in areas with poor internet connectivity. While mainstream adoption is cautious, Imani remains optimistic about bridging the gap between the need for blockchain technology and its implementation in Africa.
news.headline: African innovators are adopting blockchain to tackle real-world issues across finance, energy, and connectivity. The movement started with students, using blockchain for local needs. Beyond finance, they are also leveraging blockchain for energy solutions in Zambia. Despite cautious mainstream adoption, the technology shows promise. Listen to the full conversation on The Clear Crypto Podcast for more insights.

news.summary: In Austin, Texas, a recent survey conducted by Software Advice has revealed that cybersecurity has emerged as the primary technology investment focus for healthcare organizations in the United States. This shift in priorities comes as concerns over data breaches continue to escalate. The survey emphasizes the significant importance placed on safeguarding sensitive information within the healthcare industry.
news.headline: Healthcare organizations prioritize cybersecurity as top tech investment; survey shows rising concerns with data breach.

news.summary: Bitcoin's price is currently consolidating below its all-time high of almost $112,000, with indications pointing towards a potential surge to $120,000. Whale accumulation is on the rise, with large investors adding to their holdings as a sign of growing confidence, particularly evident in wallets holding between 100 to 1,000 BTC. In just 6 weeks, this group has added over 337 wallets and accumulated more than 122,330 BTC worth around $13.3 billion. Additionally, the Bitcoin Accumulation Trend Score sits at 1, showing intense accumulation by large investors. On top of this, US-based spot Bitcoin ETFs have seen continuous capital inflows totaling $4.2 billion over 10 consecutive days. This sustained buy-side pressure from both retail and institutional investors signals confidence in Bitcoin. The Crypto Fear and Greed Index currently stands at 74, indicating prevailing greed in the market and suggesting positive sentiment among market participants. Bitcoin futures open interest has also significantly increased to $80.5 billion from a local low of under $74,000 in April, signifying bullish environments and a buildup of leverage. The open interest in options contracts has surged to a new all-time high of $46.2 billion. There is a dense cluster of orders between $111,000 and $115,000, which might pull the price upward in the near term. Overall, the market sentiment, investor interest, and technical indicators support the potential for Bitcoin's price to reach $120,000 soon. As per predictions, Bitcoin is expected to hit $200,000 by the end of 2025.
news.headline: Bitcoin nears $112,000 ATH with signs of further growth: whales accumulate, ETF inflows strong, reaching for $120,000. Greed in market sentiment rises with positive investor interest. BTC OI hits $80.5 billion, options OI at $46.2 billion. Liquidity suggests price climb to $120,000.

news.summary: Tim Berners-Lee, the inventor of the World Wide Web and HTML, expressed his desire to make the Domain Name Service (DNS) more decentralized if he were building it today. DNS was created in 1983, replacing a previous system with a distributed naming system for IP addresses. The current DNS system is highly centralized, managed by ICANN, making censorship and service disruptions easier. Decentralized alternatives like Ethereum Name Service (ENS), Unstoppable Domains, IPFS, Handshake (HNS), Namecoin (NMC), and OpenNIC aim to provide more user-owned, censorship-resistant naming protocols. ENS has garnered adoption and partnerships with PayPal and Venmo, signaling potential mainstream acceptance. IPFS and IPNS offer mutable naming for content, not servers, promoting a decentralized approach. Overall, the push for decentralized naming systems in the crypto space seeks to address the issues of centralized control and censorship present in the current DNS infrastructure.
news.headline: Internet pioneer Tim Berners-Lee suggests more decentralization in DNS design; current system faces centralization issues, with root zone control by ICANN and potential government censorship. Decentralized alternatives like ENS and IPFS emerging to tackle problems.

news.summary: Dublin--ResearchAndMarkets.com has included the "Backup Power - Global Strategic Business Report" to its offerings. The worldwide Backup Power market was valued at US$12.2 billion in 2024 and is anticipated to hit US$16.8 billion by 2030, indicating a CAGR of 5.5% between 2024 and 2030. This detailed report offers a thorough examination of market trends, factors influencing growth, and projections, enabling informed decision-making.
news.headline: Global backup power market valued at US$12.2B in 2024, projected to reach US$16.8B by 2030, growing at 5.5% CAGR, driven by data centers and critical infrastructure expansion.

news.summary: Synopsys, Inc. reported their second-quarter 2025 earnings, with revenue at $1.60 billion, matching estimates. The adjusted EPS of $3.67 surpassed expectations of $3.38. Gross profit rose to $1.29 billion from $1.15 billion, and operating income increased to $376.4 million from $332.1 million. Synopsys CEO highlighted growth driven by AI, software-defined systems, and silicon proliferation. The company aims to address increasing design complexity and costs. US-China export curbs may impact Synopsys and Cadence due to EDA software license requirements. For more details, visit Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Synopsys (NASDAQ: SNPS) beats Q2 estimates with revenue of $1.60B and EPS of $3.67, showing growth in profit and income. CEO emphasizes their role in AI and design complexity. US tech export pressure poses a challenge.

news.summary: Agilent Technologies Inc. announced its second-quarter 2025 sales of $1.67 billion, closely aligning with the $1.63 billion consensus and marking a 6% growth reported, with a 5.3% core year-over-year increase. The Life Sciences and Diagnostics Markets Group (LDG) recorded second-quarter revenue of $654 million, showing an 8% growth reported and a 3% core year-over-year rise, with an operating margin of 19.7%. The Agilent CrossLab Group (ACG) reported $713 million in revenue for the quarter, reflecting a 7% reported growth and a 9% core year-over-year increase, with an operating margin of 32.4%. The Applied Markets Group (AMG) had second-quarter revenue of $301 million, indicating a 1% reported decrease, remaining flat core year-over-year, with an operating margin of 19.5%. Keep up with financial news on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Agilent's Q2 2025 sales hit $1.67B, beating consensus of $1.63B. LDG saw $654M, 8% growth, ACG $713M up 7%, AMG $301M down 1%.

news.summary: ELF Beauty's stock surged following a successful earnings report and a significant announcement about acquiring Hailey Bieber's skincare brand Rhode in a $1 billion deal. This move has propelled ELF Beauty's stock into a positive trajectory, while also putting competitor Ulta in a purchase zone. This news was covered in an article by Investor's Business Daily.
news.headline: ELF Beauty stock surges on earnings beat, announces $1 billion deal for Hailey Bieber's skincare brand Rhode.

news.summary: Galxe has recently unveiled their new community engagement platform, "Starboard," but this launch is not a drastic pivot for the company. They have successfully brought in 34 million users to Web3 through their Quest platform. With the introduction of Starboard, they aim to shift their focus from customer onboarding to community management. Galxe's Head of GTM, Patrick Young, emphasizes that Starboard enhances their capabilities by providing projects with more insights into their communities to drive substantial growth. Starboard is positioned as unique from competitors such as Layer3, Zealy, and RabbitHole. It monitors client progression through an "Aura" score, which considers off-chain metrics like social media engagement, in addition to on-chain data like trading volume. For more details, please refer to the complete article on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Galxe launches new community building platform "Starboard" after onboarding 34 million users; aims to enhance customer onboarding into community management.

news.summary: SentinelOne Inc (NYSE: S) announced better-than-expected first-quarter sales, revealing a revenue of $229.03 million, surpassing the estimated $228.35 million. Their adjusted earnings for the quarter were at two cents per share, meeting analyst predictions. CEO Tomer Weingarten expressed satisfaction with the company's growth and margin improvement, attributing it to platform momentum and customer satisfaction. Weingarten also highlighted their innovation strategy, emphasizing advancements in AI, Data, Cloud, and Endpoint technologies. SentinelOne anticipates second-quarter revenue to be around $242 million, slightly below the expected $244.88 million.
news.headline: SentinelOne beats Q1 revenue estimate with $229.03M; forecasts $242M for Q2, slightly below estimates. CEO optimistic about company's growth and innovation.

news.summary: In New York, The Estée Lauder Companies Inc. (NYSE: EL) has appointed Lisa Sequino as the new President of the Makeup Brand Cluster. She will be in charge of guiding the strategic direction and global expansion of makeup, managing the company’s range of makeup brands - M·A·C, Bobbi Brown, Too Faced, Smashbox, and GLAMGLOW. Reporting to Jane Hertzmark Hudis, Executive Vice President and Chief Brand Officer, Ms. Sequino will officially assume her role in June.
news.headline: Lisa Sequino appointed as President, Makeup Brand Cluster at Estée Lauder Companies overseeing brands like MAC, Bobbi Brown, Too Faced.

news.summary: SafelyYou, a company specializing in enhancing safety and person-centered care in senior living facilities through advanced AI technology, groundbreaking hardware, and remote clinical support, recently shared that its CEO George Netscher has been appointed to the Board of Directors for Argentum. Argentum is a prominent national association focused on assisting organizations that manage senior living communities with professionalism and resident-focused care. news.summary: The article discusses strategies for identifying promising cryptocurrency projects early, avoiding hype-driven investments, and spotting potential red flags. It highlights successful projects like Solana, Arbitrum, Chainlink, and PEPE Coin, showcasing indicators such as onchain metrics, tokenomics, developer activity, and community engagement. It emphasizes the importance of analyzing real signals over manufactured buzz and provides tips on how to differentiate between genuine growth and inflated hype. Tools like Dune Analytics, Nansen, DefiLlama, LunarCrush, and Santiment are recommended for tracking trends. The article also lists key factors for identifying crypto gems early and outlines signs of both real traction and manufactured hype. It warns investors of red flags such as high holder concentration, unverified token contracts, lack of liquidity lock, and upcoming big token unlocks. The emphasis is on conducting thorough research, verifying information, and looking beyond surface level indicators to make informed investment decisions in the volatile cryptocurrency market.
news.headline: Discovering 100x crypto projects ahead of the hype requires analyzing onchain data, tokenomics, dev activity, and community traction. Explore lessons from past successes like Solana, Arbitrum, Chainlink, and Pepe Coin to find potential winners early. Key factors include onchain metrics, tokenomics, developer activity, ecosystem signals, and community engagement. Tools like Dune Analytics, Nansen, and GitHub are crucial for trendspotters. Watch for real traction indicators like steady growth in users, code commits, ecosystem activity, and liquidity. Be cautious of manufactured hype signs such as sudden social spikes or lack of dev activity. Check for red flags like high holder concentration, unverified contracts, and big token unlocks. Spotting crypto trends before the crowd involves thorough research and cross-referencing various data sources to distinguish substance from noise.

news.summary: Foot Locker Inc (NYSE: FL) released its first-quarter results and announced a definitive acquisition agreement by Dick's Sporting Goods Inc (NYSE: DKS), valuing Foot Locker's equity at approximately $2.4 billion and its enterprise at around $2.5 billion. The company reported a 4.6% year-on-year decline in quarterly sales to $1.79 billion, falling short of the analyst consensus of $1.89 billion. Comparable store sales dropped by 2.6% compared to the previous year. Additionally, the adjusted EPS stood at 7 cents. For more details, refer to the complete story on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Foot Locker reports 4.6% sales decline in Q1, now being acquired by Dick's Sporting Goods for $2.4B.

news.summary: NVIDIA Corp (NASDAQ: NVDA) exceeded expectations in the first quarter, reporting revenue of $44.06 billion, surpassing the estimated $43.21 billion. Adjusted earnings were 81 cents per share, or 96 cents per share excluding charges related to chip sales restrictions in China, which was higher than anticipated. Total revenue increased by 69% compared to the previous year. Data center revenue was $39.1 billion, up 73%, while Gaming & AI PC revenue reached $3.8 billion, up 42%. Professional Visualization revenue stood at $509 million, up 19%, and Automotive & Robotics revenue amounted to $567 million, up 72%. Analysts have maintained Buy ratings or increased price targets following these results. Nvidia will pay a quarterly cash dividend of one cent per share on July 3 to shareholders as of June 11.
news.headline: Nvidia stock surges after beating Q1 earnings with revenue of $44.06 billion, up 69% YoY, led by data center growth.

news.summary: In Bridgewater, New Jersey, statistics reveal that menopause is both a medical and personal journey for women, often marked by emotions like shame and confusion. Many women feel neglected and undervalued by the healthcare system as they navigate this transition. The HRT Club, a prominent provider of direct-to-consumer hormone replacement therapies, recently shared data indicating that 32.8% of peri/menopausal American women express interest in hormone replacement therapy. This demonstrates a significant consumer demand for such treatments, highlighting the need for improved support and understanding during menopause.
news.headline: Costs rise & confidence falls, 93% of women demand change in menopause support. New data by The HRT Club: 32.8% American peri/menopausal face dismissal.
